MinMaxScaler.fit_transform(data[numerical]) at line 126 is fit on the full dataset, and pd.get_dummies(features_raw) at line 151 is applied to the full dataset — both occur before train_test_split (line 176), so test distribution leaks into scaling parameters and encoding columns

X_test reused repeatedly with no independent holdout: lines 307/367 (3 classifiers x 3 sample sizes = 9 evaluations for model selection), lines 425-440 (GridSearchCV best_clf vs unoptimized clf comparison), lines 513-528 (reduced-feature model comparison) — same test set drives multiple modeling decisions

CountVectorizer fit/fit_transform on the entire critics.quote corpus before any train/test split (split happens inside train_and_measure at line 107) — vocabulary and term counts from future test rows leak into the feature matrix

train_and_measure() called repeatedly (>=50 times: 3 classifiers at lines 117/120/123, F-selected features at 277, a 50-iteration percentile loop at 307-318, POS-tag features at 386), each time re-splitting with the same random_state=1234 and comparing test accuracy to pick the best classifier/feature subset — no independent final test set is held out

f_classif(x,y) ANOVA F-test computed on the full feature matrix x and full label vector y (not train-only) to build a feature-selection mask (line 269, repeated each loop iteration at 308); selected features are then used for both training and testing, so test-set distribution informs which features are kept

find_k() returns a classifier fit on the last KFold training fold (line 175); this clf is then evaluated via predict/confusion_matrix/predict_proba on the FULL x (lines 185, 195, 203) which includes rows the model was trained on — accuracy and confusion matrix are partly computed on training data

Second CountVectorizer fit_transform on critics.pos applied to the full corpus before the line 386 split inside train_and_measure — same vocabulary-leakage pattern as lines 85-86, repeated for POS-tag features

TfidfVectorizer.fit_transform() called on the full X_train_pos["text"] column before train_test_split (line 104) — IDF weights are computed using documents that later end up in the test split (same pattern also present but unused for the X_train_norm/X_train_sw branches at lines 47 and 54)

Nine different regression models (Linear, Ridge, Lasso, 3x KNN, DecisionTree, RandomForest, AdaBoost, GradientBoosting) are all fit on the same X_train/y_train and evaluated on the same X_test/y_test to compare RMSE — no independent held-out test set is reserved, so the single split functions as a validation set across repeated model-selection comparisons

patsy.dmatrix() builds the categorical design matrix (C(city)+C(state)+C(company)+C(Title_New)) from the full wdf dataframe before the line 354 train_test_split — dummy/category columns are determined using categories present in rows that later become the test set

GridSearchCV.fit(X, y) is called on the FULL feature matrix and label vector (X, y from lines 342-343), not on X_train/Y_train (created at line 354) — test rows are included in the cross-validation folds used to select the best hyperparameters (C, penalty)

X_test/Y_test reused for two separate evaluation rounds: the unoptimized LogisticRegression (lines 367-409: predictions, confusion matrix, classification report, ROC curve) and again for the GridSearchCV-tuned model (lines 439-453: predictions, confusion matrix, classification report) — same test set drives the optimized-vs-unoptimized comparison with no independent final test set

Kaggle train+test concatenated into all_data (line 32-33) before all preprocessing; pd.get_dummies(all_data) (line 68) and all_data.fillna(all_data.mean()) (line 75) are both fit on the combined train+test frame, then split back via slicing (lines 82-84) — competition test distribution leaks into one-hot columns and imputed means, the canonical Kaggle concat-before-preprocess pattern

StandardScaler().fit_transform(X_train) (line 240) is fit on the full X_train before it is split into X_tr/X_val at line 246 for the Keras model — scaling statistics leak from the validation rows into the scaler used for training

A per-column MinMaxScaler((-1,1)) applied via r.apply(f, axis=0) (lines 44-49) is fit on the full final dataframe r (reassigned at line 42, after dropna at line 43) before train_test_split at line 57 — scaling min/max are computed using rows that later become the test set

X_test reused to evaluate 7 different classifiers in sequence (6 SGDClassifier loss variants in the loop at lines 64-72, plus GaussianNB at lines 73-81) — same test set drives model comparison/selection with no independent final test set

Imputer(strategy="median").fit_transform(data_.values) (line 78), f_regression/mutual_info_regression (lines 97, 229) and critically SelectPercentile(mutual_info_regression).fit(X, rent.values) (line 301) are all computed on the FULL dataset before the first train_test_split (line 349) — the resulting X_selected (line 314) carries this feature-selection leakage into every downstream split in the notebook

pd.get_dummies(grr, columns=grr.columns, ...) (line 581) is applied to the full X_selected_ dataset before the train_test_split at lines 588-589 — repeats the same pre-split full-data encoding pattern as lines 78-314

X_val/y_val reused across dozens of distinct models and feature/target engineering variants (RandomForest 357-358, LinearRegression 372-385, OHE pipelines 399-419, polynomial-interaction pipelines 503-558, box-cox/power-transformed targets 635-661, dummy-encoded subset 694-711, final "asdf" pipeline 729-748) for model selection — meanwhile X_test/y_test (created at lines 349, 490, 588, 687) are never evaluated against any model, so no independent final test score is ever reported
